name: Cardeña — round trip to the Embalse de las Tejoneras
name_original: Cardeña — ida y vuelta por el Embalse de las Tejoneras
region_intro: Hiking in Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by gentle hills and rugged terrain with steep walls and wide valleys carved by rivers like the Yeguas and Arenoso. The region features distinctive batholithic granite formations, often appearing as striking boulders. Vegetation includes extensive dehesa woodlands of Holm and Cork oaks, Mediterranean scrubland, and unique oak forests. These varied features provide a rich environment for waterfall hiking trails. Best waterfall hiking trails in Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro The most popular waterfall hiking route is La Batanera Rock Art Site – Batanera Waterfall loop from Plaza de toros, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, passing by a rock art site and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada de la batanera y el Río Cereceda — circular por la Sierra de Dornilleros, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.4 km) path. This circular route explores the Sierra de Dornilleros, featuring a waterfall and the Cereceda River. Local hikers also love the Batanera Waterfall – La Batanera Rock Art Site loop from Parking La Batanera, an 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a waterfall and rock art site, often completed in about 5 hours 41 minutes. Hiking in Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro is defined by dehesa woodlands, granite formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from around 15 reviews. More than 120 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra De Cardeña y Montoro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: There are currently 3 dedicated waterfall hiking trails documented in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, for an easy and accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site – Batanera Waterfall loop from Plaza de toros&lt;/a&gt;. This 5 km trail is generally completed in under 1.5 hours and passes by both a rock art site and the Batanera Waterfall, making it a great choice for a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on waterfall hikes in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The terrain in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro is quite varied, featuring dehesa woodlands, distinctive batholithic granite formations, and river valleys. You'll encounter a mix of gentle hills and more rugged sections, especially near the rivers like the Yeguas and Arenoso that carve through the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes in the area?
A: Yes, a popular circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197231413/cascada-de-la-batanera-y-el-rio-cereceda-circular-por-la-sierra-de-dornilleros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la batanera y el Río Cereceda — circular por la Sierra de Dornilleros&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 10.4 km path explores the Sierra de Dornilleros, offering views of the Batanera Waterfall and following the Cereceda River.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The trails in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from 17 reviews. Hikers often praise the natural beauty, the unique granite formations, and the peaceful atmosphere of the dehesa woodlands.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls themselves, you can explore several interesting spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo Bridge over the Yeguas River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5639835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path from Venta del Charco to El Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; also provides a glimpse into the region's cultural heritage.

Q: Is there a longer, more challenging waterfall hike for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more demanding adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall – La Batanera Rock Art Site loop from Parking La Batanera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.2 km trail. This route leads through varied terrain to the Batanera Waterfall and a rock art site, typically taking around 5 hours and 40 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking near the waterfalls?
A: Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro is a haven for wildlife. While hiking, you might spot red deer, fallow deer, and wild boar. The park is also a critical habitat for elusive species like the Iberian Lynx and Iberian Wolf, and you may see various birds of prey such as golden eagles and black vultures soaring overhead.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls in Sierra De Cardeña Y Montoro?
A: The waterfalls are generally at their most impressive during the wetter seasons, typically late autumn, winter, and early spring, when rainfall is higher. During these times, the rivers are fuller, enhancing the flow and spectacle of the cascades.

Q: Are there any specific geological features to look out for on these trails?
A: Yes, the park is known for its distinctive batholithic granite formations, often appearing as striking boulders known as 'bolos'. These unique rock features add to the scenic beauty and geological interest of the hiking trails, particularly in areas carved by the Yeguas and Arenoso rivers.

Q: What are the main types of vegetation I will see on a waterfall hike?
A: You'll primarily experience diverse Mediterranean ecosystems. Expect to walk through extensive dehesa woodlands dominated by Holm and Cork oaks, as well as areas of Mediterranean scrubland with species like kermes oak, rockroses, and wild olives. Unique oak forests, including Pyrenean and Gall oaks, can also be found in more humid sections.
tips: Entry point to the Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro Natural Park, it is the highest municipality above sea level in the province of Córdoba, at 821 m. In its surroundings, and to a greater extent as you enter the Natural Park, you can find holm oaks, gall oaks, wild olive trees, cork oaks and Pyrenean oaks.
Reservoir that is located in the vicinity of Cardeña and that is surrounded by devesas typical of this western area of the Sierra d'Andújar Natural Park.
